title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
Total member shares stood at $845 million as of March 31, 2026, representing a 1.2% increase from the prior quarter.

Share drafts constituted the largest funding component at $253 million (29.9%), followed by share certificates at $254 million (30.1%). Regular shares represented 24.7%, money market shares accounted for 12.4%, IRA/Keogh accounts comprised 2.8%, all other shares totaled $0.5 million, and nonmember deposits were negligible.

Share drafts increased by 6.0% over the quarter, while share certificates grew at a modest 1.5%. Regular shares expanded significantly with a 7.9% quarterly gain. Money market shares declined slightly by -1.7%, whereas IRA/Keogh accounts remained flat and nonmember deposits saw minimal growth.

The combined strength of regular shares and share drafts indicates that the credit union's funding mix is moving toward more flexible, lower-cost sources while maintaining a substantial base in fixed-term certificates.
